danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive boss sprite with unique color scheme and mechanical design at stage end
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring combat engagement; arena transition signals imminent danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, pits, moving platforms, and environmental obstacles with contrasting colors
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger that can damage or kill Mega Man; avoidable through platforming skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Red/dark-colored minion robots appearing in stage layouts
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; indicates hostile territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon/item energy bar decreasing; sprite flickering when health is critical

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating energy orbs (red for health, blue/yellow for weapon energy) from defeated enemies
Mega Man sprite reaching stage exit/door at stage terminus
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone reached; transition to boss arena or next content
Progress checkpoint; reduced consequence for failure

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red/dark purple dominant palette for Robot Masters and minions
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wily's distinctive castle architecture; mechanical robot design aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each Robot Master has unique sprite and color identity (e.g., Quick Man in blue, Fire Man in orange/red); stage themed around master's weapon type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wily's fortress/castle structures; industrial mechanical environments; stages reflect Robot Master elemental/conceptual theme

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue with red/yellow accents on suit
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Man sprite design; Rush (robot dog) companion; acquired weapons visually distinct from enemy weapons
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mega Man maintains consistent blue suit across all games; special weapons glow with distinct colors when equipped
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Safe zones; weapon/item drops in Mega Man's color palette

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct new color schemes based on Journey to the West character designs
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Buster Rod.G (Sun Wukong appearance), Mega Water.S (water-themed), Hyper Storm.H (storm-themed)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: New boss designs distinct from original Robot Masters; classical Chinese visual influence in sprite design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wily Tower structure; fusion environment combining elements from prior stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen and narrative setup showing Dr. Wily's time machine invention
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mega Man enters time travel sequence; player navigates through recreated historical battles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16-bit remakes of original NES stages with enhanced Genesis-era graphics and arranged music
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Same stage layouts and Robot Master encounters as originals; player must relive and overcome past challenges with accumulated knowledge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New tower structure; Genesis Unit boss designs; environment integrating elements from all three time periods
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must synthesize weapons and items from all three games; final confrontation with Dr. Wily in tower

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage-by-stage visual evolution as player defeats Robot Masters; weapon selection menu shows acquired abilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mega Man gains new weapon after each boss; player can select and swap between acquired special weapons

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage theming reflects Robot Master identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Fire Man's stage features flame-colored obstacles and heat-themed hazards; Quick Man's stage uses speed-based platforming challenges

environmental_storytelling.technique: Visual upgrade quality communicates technological advancement
environmental_storytelling.example: 16-bit graphical enhancement from NES originals demonstrates time passing and world evolution through Wily's technological iterations

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy and obstacle placement creates environmental narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Robot Master minions appear throughout stage, establishing that Wily's forces occupy the territory; stage design progression shows escalating difficulty toward boss confrontation
Defeating Robot Masters grants visual representation of conquest; weapon menu becomes fuller, visually demonstrating player capability growth
Tower structure incorporates visual elements from all three time periods, suggesting Wily's cumulative experimental knowledge and power escalation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Rapid movement, basic shot attacks; vulnerable to all hazards and enemy fire equally

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Equipped with special Robot Master weapon
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Special weapon attack animation distinct from Buster; modified movement speed based on weapon load; specific weapons effective against certain bosses
Sprite flickers; reduced attack effectiveness; heightened vulnerability
Attack pattern specific to Robot Master identity; stage-specific movement constraints; telegraphed attack animations
Sprite explosion/destruction animation; weapon item drop; stage clear transition
Predictable movement patterns; spawn from designated locations; attack on player proximity
Multi-phase boss battle; requires synthesis of player-acquired weapons; escalating attack complexity

notes: Mega Man: The Wily Wars communicates world-state primarily through stage-by-stage progression and Robot Master visual distinctiveness. The game uses color-coded faction identity (blue for Mega Man, red/dark for Wily forces) and environmental theming to establish danger and allegiance. The narrative framing of time-travel replay establishes a unique meta-world-state where past conflicts are replayed with present knowledge. The unlockable Wily Tower serves as a world-state transition marker, visually and mechanically signaling culmination of player progress. Enemy behavior tells are largely predetermined/scripted rather than reactive, maintaining classic Mega Man series design. The 16-bit visual upgrade itself communicates a passage of time and technological progression within the narrative frame.
